subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Sun's Future
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Sun's Future.

hook.text: The Sun is halfway through its life. In 5 billion years, it will exhaust its hydrogen fuel.

buildup.text: When hydrogen runs low, the core contracts and heats up. Helium begins fusing into carbon and oxygen.

discovery.text: The outer layers expand, turning the Sun into a red giant 200 times its current size.

twist.text: After shedding its outer layers as a colorful nebula, only the dense core remains—a white dwarf.

climax.text: That white dwarf will cool over trillions of years, fading into a cold, dark cinder.

punchline.text: Born in a cloud, dies as a cinder. A star's full arc.

quiz.question: What will the Sun ultimately become after it exhausts its fuel?
options: A white dwarf that slowly cools into darkness
A black hole that devours the solar system
A neutron star spinning thousands of times per second
